an4.1:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying among the Vajjians at Bhaṇḍa Village.
an4.1:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
an4.1:2.1: “It’s because of not understanding and not penetrating four things that we have transmigrated & wandered on for such a long, long time, you & I.
an4.1:2.3: “It’s because of not understanding and not penetrating noble virtue that we have transmigrated & wandered on for such a long, long time, you & I.
an4.1:2.4: “It’s because of not understanding and not penetrating noble concentration that we have transmigrated & wandered on for such a long, long time, you & I.
an4.1:2.5: “It’s because of not understanding and not penetrating noble discernment that we have transmigrated & wandered on for such a long, long time, you & I.
an4.1:2.6: “It’s because of not understanding and not penetrating noble release that we have transmigrated & wandered on for such a long, long time, you & I.
an4.1:2.7: “But now that noble virtue is understood & penetrated, noble concentration… noble discernment… noble release is understood & penetrated, craving for becoming is destroyed, the guide to becoming [craving] is ended, there is now no further becoming.”
an4.1:3.1: That is what the Blessed One said.
an4.1:3.2: When the One Well-Gone had said that, he—the Teacher—said further:
an4.1:5.1: he taught the Dhamma to the monks—
an4.1:5.3: the Teacher who has put an end to suffering & stress,
an4.1:5.4: the One with vision totally unbound.
